_provenance: Claude-authored original Gaokao seven-select. Not copied. DeepSeek not used.

sets.passage: Friendship is something almost everyone claims to want, and yet very few people ever stop to think carefully about what actually holds a true friendship together over many long and changing years. (1)______ Without it, two people may share countless meals, jokes, and pleasant afternoons together, and still remain little more than comfortable strangers who simply happen to spend a great deal of their time near each other. Trust, of course, is rarely built in a single dramatic moment of grand sacrifice or sudden heroic loyalty. (2)______ Instead, it grows quietly and almost invisibly through dozens of tiny, ordinary choices, such as keeping a small promise, returning a borrowed book on time, or simply showing up faithfully when you said that you would. One of the quickest and saddest ways to damage a friendship, on the other hand, is to share a private secret that you were clearly asked to keep entirely to yourself. (3)______ Even when the gossip seems harmless and the temptation to tell feels almost impossible to resist, that single careless slip can leave a deep crack that no later apology will ever fully repair. Real friends also gradually learn the difficult and delicate art of honest disagreement without any trace of cruelty. (4)______ They understand, through long experience, that telling a kind but uncomfortable truth is often a far greater gift than offering an easy, empty compliment that helps nobody at all to grow. Perhaps the most overlooked ingredient of all, however, is plain everyday patience with each other's faults, moods, and difficult days. (5)______ Everyone, after all, occasionally arrives late, forgets an important date, or speaks more sharply than they truly meant to, and a friendship strong enough to survive such small private storms is surely worth protecting with real and lasting care.
bank: A close friend can disagree with you firmly and openly and still leave you feeling completely respected.
At the very heart of every lasting bond lies one quiet quality that we too often take for granted.
Buying generous and expensive gifts for each other is by far the surest way to keep any friendship alive.
We tend to forgive in a good friend exactly the kind of small mistakes we secretly hope they will forgive in us.
The most loyal friends, some people wrongly believe, simply agree with everything you say to avoid any conflict.
Once such confidence has been carelessly broken, it becomes extremely hard to feel fully safe with that person again.
It is, in plain truth, the steady and unglamorous result of small actions repeated patiently across a great many days.
sets.explain_zh: 空1选B（点出友谊核心在于信任这一被忽视的品质）；空2选G（信任靠日积月累的小事建立）；空3选F（泄密后信任难以恢复，承接守密话题）；空4选A（朋友能坦诚分歧又互相尊重）；空5选D（彼此包容缺点，呼应耐心）。干扰句C（送礼）、E（一味附和）放入任一空都不连贯。

sets.passage: When people first hear the phrase climate change, many of them immediately picture a single dramatic disaster, such as a collapsing glacier or a flooded coastal city far away from their own quiet daily lives. (1)______ In truth, the slow warming of our planet shows itself mainly through small, quiet shifts that are remarkably easy to overlook from one ordinary year to the very next. Farmers, for example, are often among the very first people anywhere to notice that something is steadily and stubbornly going wrong. (2)______ A growing season that once began reliably in early spring may now arrive several weeks earlier or later, leaving crops confused, soil unprepared, and harvests far harder to predict than they used to be. Cities, too, face their own particular set of growing troubles as average summer temperatures continue to creep slowly but relentlessly upward year after year. (3)______ Concrete and dark asphalt soak up the strong sun throughout the day and then release that stored heat slowly through the night, so crowded urban summers grow steadily more dangerous for the elderly and the very young. Yet the overall picture, fortunately, is not one of pure hopelessness or quiet despair. (4)______ Many ordinary towns and neighbourhoods have already begun planting more shade trees, painting their roofs in cooler reflective colours, and protecting the wetlands that quietly absorb rising floodwaters before they ever reach people's homes. The single most important thing to remember, perhaps, is that small daily choices genuinely do add up over time. (5)______ When millions of separate households each waste a little less energy and clearly demand cleaner power from those who govern them, the combined effect slowly becomes large enough to bend the curve of our shared and uncertain future.
bank: Buying a larger and far more powerful car each single year is the best response any worried family can make.
They watch the timing of rain, frost, and summer heat drift slowly away from the patterns their parents once trusted.
Across the whole world, ordinary communities are quietly proving that thoughtful, patient action can still make a real difference.
This common mental image, however striking it may be, captures only a tiny corner of a far broader and slower problem.
Heat becomes trapped between tall buildings in a way that the open countryside almost never experiences at all.
Scientists everywhere now agree that the climate has always been perfectly stable and will certainly never change at all.
No single person can ever hope to fix the whole planet, but no honest effort is ever completely wasted either.
sets.explain_zh: 空1选D（纠正人们对气候变化的片面印象）；空2选B（农民最先察觉气候规律漂移）；空3选E（城市热岛效应，承接城市升温）；空4选C（社区行动带来希望）；空5选G（个人微小努力汇聚成改变）。干扰句A（买大车）、F（气候一直稳定）放入任一空都不连贯。

sets.passage: Music is one of the very few human creations that seems to speak directly to the human heart, often reaching us long before our slow reasoning minds have had any chance at all to catch up. (1)______ A single half-forgotten melody can carry us instantly back to a warm summer afternoon from early childhood, or to the face of a person we have not actually seen in many long and quiet years. Scientists who carefully study the human brain have found that this strange power is far from accidental or imagined. (2)______ When we listen closely to a piece of music that we love, several different regions of the brain light up all at once, releasing the very same pleasant chemicals that reward us after eating a good meal or laughing with close friends. Music also has a truly remarkable ability to draw separate, unrelated strangers together into a single shared feeling. (3)______ Anyone who has ever stood in a packed and roaring stadium, singing the very same words as thousands of people they have never once met, knows that strange and powerful sense of sudden belonging. Beyond pure pleasure, music can also serve as a genuine and surprising tool for healing tired and deeply troubled minds. (4)______ Trained therapists now use carefully chosen songs and gentle, steady rhythms to help patients recover their speech after a stroke, to calm anxious children before frightening surgery, and to comfort the very old as their memory slowly fades. Perhaps the most wonderful thing about music of all, however, is the simple fact that it truly belongs to absolutely everyone. (5)______ You need no expensive instrument and no formal training at all to hum a tune in the shower, to tap a steady beat on a kitchen table, or to feel your whole tired spirit quietly lifted by a song on the radio.
bank: Loud music in public places should always be strictly banned because it disturbs serious and hardworking people.
A shared rhythm can dissolve the invisible walls between people far faster than almost any long spoken argument.
Listening to a deeply beloved song activates the brain's own reward system in much the same way that pleasure itself does.
Its emotional reach is so surprisingly deep that just a few notes can stir memories we firmly believed were long forgotten.
Only those rare people born with unusual natural talent can ever truly enjoy or fully understand what music has to offer.
From the very youngest crying baby to the oldest grandparent, almost everyone responds in some way to a moving sound.
Hospitals around the world are increasingly discovering that sound itself can ease real pain and slowly rebuild damaged connections.
sets.explain_zh: 空1选D（音乐情感力量极深，几个音符唤起记忆）；空2选C（脑科学证明听爱曲激活奖赏系统）；空3选B（共享节奏拉近陌生人）；空4选G（医院用声音减痛、修复，承接疗愈）；空5选F（音乐属于每个人，老少皆有反应）。干扰句A（禁噪音）、E（仅天才才懂）放入任一空都不连贯。

sets.passage: Almost everyone agrees, at least in comfortable theory, that eating well is genuinely important, and yet turning that simple agreement into a steady daily habit turns out to be surprisingly difficult for most ordinary people. (1)______ The crowded shelves of every supermarket are stacked with bright, cheerful packages that loudly promise quick energy and wonderful taste, while quietly hiding far too much sugar, salt, and hidden fat deep inside. A genuinely healthy diet does not, however, require strange expensive powders or an endless and gloomy list of forbidden foods. (2)______ In most cases it simply means filling rather more of your plate with fresh vegetables, fruits, whole grains, and beans, while treating heavy, processed snacks as occasional welcome guests rather than as permanent daily residents. The exact way in which we eat often matters very nearly as much as what we actually choose to put on the plate. (3)______ Wolfing down a large heavy meal in five rushed minutes while staring at a glowing screen leaves the tired body very little chance to notice that it has, in fact, already had quite enough. Cooking real food at home, though it certainly takes a little more effort and time, gives you a kind of quiet control that no busy restaurant ever truly can. (4)______ When you chop the vegetables and carefully measure the oil with your own two hands, you know exactly what is going into your body, and you slowly rebuild a healthy respect for where real food actually comes from. None of this, importantly, demands cold perfection or harsh self-punishment for every small slip along the way. (5)______ A single slice of birthday cake or a generous plate of holiday dumplings will never truly ruin an otherwise sensible pattern of eating built up patiently across the ordinary weeks of a whole long and busy year.
bank: Skipping every meal entirely for several days in a row is by far the fastest and safest way to become genuinely healthy.
Part of the real trouble is that the cheapest and most convenient foods are often the very least nourishing of all.
Eating slowly and thoughtfully allows your patient stomach the time it badly needs to tell your brain that you are full.
The most expensive diet plan on the whole market is absolutely guaranteed to work far better than any home cooking ever could.
Instead, gentle balance over a long stretch of time matters far more than any single perfect or imperfect meal.
Most of what your body truly needs can in fact be found in ordinary, affordable ingredients that you already know well.
Preparing your own simple meals quietly connects you once again to the plain ingredients hidden inside everything you taste.
sets.explain_zh: 空1选B（点出便宜方便食品最不营养这一困难）；空2选F（健康饮食靠平价普通食材）；空3选C（细嚼慢咽让大脑感知饱足，承接吃法）；空4选G（自己做饭重新连接食材）；空5选E（长期平衡胜过单餐完美）。干扰句A（绝食）、D（昂贵食谱）放入任一空都不连贯。

sets.passage: In our modern age of endless notifications and glowing screens, the simple human ability to focus on one single thing at a time has quietly become one of the rarest and most valuable skills that a person can possibly possess. (1)______ Every buzzing phone, every blinking message, and every newly opened tab gently pulls our scattered attention off in yet another direction, until, by the tired end of the day, we feel completely exhausted and yet strangely unsure of what we have actually managed to accomplish. Many people proudly and confidently believe that they are genuinely skilled at doing several demanding things all at once. (2)______ In hard reality, the human brain does not truly run two difficult tasks together in parallel; it merely switches rapidly back and forth between them, paying a small hidden cost in lost time and accuracy with every single jump. The genuinely good news is that deep concentration, much like an ordinary muscle, can be patiently trained and steadily strengthened over time. (3)______ Setting aside a fixed block of perhaps thirty quiet, protected minutes, with the phone left in another room entirely, often produces far more real progress than three long but scattered hours of constant interruption. The physical space around us also shapes our daily focus far more powerfully than we usually like to admit to ourselves. (4)______ A clean, tidy desk holding only the single task in front of you sends a quiet but clear signal to the restless mind that now, at long last, is the proper time for serious and undivided work. Above all, it helps enormously to remember that our attention is a strictly limited daily resource, very much like a small sum of money carried in a purse. (5)______ Every single time we choose to spend it carelessly on some shallow passing distraction, we have a little less of it left over for the deeper, harder things that genuinely matter most of all to us.
bank: Keeping ten or more browser tabs open at all times is supposedly the secret habit of every truly productive person.
What we so proudly call multitasking is, the careful research now strongly suggests, mostly an expensive and tiring illusion.
The constant flood of these small interruptions slowly fragments our serious thinking into countless tiny, scattered, useless pieces.
Spending it all carelessly in the early morning leaves us much poorer and far less capable for the rest of the long day.
Visible clutter sitting within our line of sight constantly tempts the restless eye away toward a dozen unfinished and unrelated jobs.
Beginning with short, fiercely protected sessions and then slowly extending them over weeks is exactly how that mental strength grows.
The more separate screens you bravely try to watch all at once, the deeper and clearer your concentration is certain to become.
sets.explain_zh: 空1选C（小打扰把思维切成碎片，承接专注稀缺）；空2选B（所谓多任务实为昂贵幻觉）；空3选F（用短时段循序训练专注力）；空4选E（视野杂乱诱使分心，承接物理环境）；空5选D（注意力像金钱，乱花则后劲不足）。干扰句A（开十个标签页）、G（多屏反而更专注）放入任一空都不连贯。

sets.passage: Insects are so small and so endlessly common that most people walk straight past many millions of them every single ordinary day without ever once sparing them a serious second thought. (1)______ Yet this vast, buzzing, crawling, often hidden world quietly performs a whole range of jobs so deeply essential that human life as we now know it could not possibly continue for very long without them. Consider, first of all, the simple everyday matter of the food that appears on our tables. (2)______ Bees, butterflies, beetles, and countless other tiny tireless visitors carry pollen patiently from one flower to the next, quietly allowing a huge share of all our fruits, nuts, and vegetables to form at all. Insects also serve, perhaps less pleasantly, as nature's own tireless, unpaid, and largely invisible cleaning crew. (3)______ Beetles, ants, and flies steadily break down fallen leaves, dead animals, and all kinds of natural waste, returning their precious nutrients to the soil so that fresh new life can rise again from the old. They form, in addition to all this, the broad foundation of countless food chains across very nearly every habitat on dry land. (4)______ Birds, fish, frogs, and a great many small mammals would all quickly starve and disappear without the steady, reliable supply of protein that this enormous insect population faithfully provides them every day. Sadly, scientists right across the world now warn that insect numbers are falling at a frightening, unnatural, and quite unprecedented speed. (5)______ Planting native flowers, using far fewer harsh chemicals in our gardens, and leaving small wild corners completely untouched are all simple, practical steps that can genuinely help these tiny, overlooked workers survive into the uncertain future.
bank: They quietly remove and recycle what would otherwise pile up and slowly rot in great, unbearable, towering heaps.
Most of us carelessly dismiss them as little more than annoying pests to be swatted away or quietly ignored.
Killing every single insect that we possibly can would quite obviously make the whole natural world far cleaner and healthier.
Almost every other creature higher up the food chain depends, directly or indirectly, on insects for its very survival.
Spiders, by themselves, are somehow responsible for producing nearly all of the oxygen that humans quietly breathe every day.
The exact reasons are complex and tangled, but ordinary people can still do a great deal to slow this alarming decline.
Without these patient and unpaid pollinators, our crowded supermarkets would suddenly look far emptier and grimmer than they do today.
sets.explain_zh: 空1填索引1（人们视昆虫为害虫而忽视，承接小且常见）；空2填索引6（传粉者支撑餐桌食物）；空3填索引0（昆虫清除腐烂废物，承接清洁队）；空4填索引3（昆虫是食物链基础，上层生物依赖它们）；空5填索引5（数量锐减但普通人能助其存活，收尾呼应）。干扰句索引2（灭绝昆虫更好）、索引4（蜘蛛产氧）放入任一空都不连贯。

sets.passage: For many long years the whole story of the Chinese countryside seemed to move in only one steady and rather sad direction, as ambitious young people left their quiet home villages in search of work in distant cities and the old family houses slowly emptied out behind them. (1)______ Recently, however, a quieter and far more genuinely hopeful chapter has gradually begun to unfold across a great many rural regions of the country. One especially powerful change has been the steady, almost unnoticed arrival of fast and reliable internet even in remote and once-isolated mountain valleys. (2)______ A modest farmer who once sold his apples only to a single passing trader can now reach eager customers in distant cities directly through a small phone, often nearly doubling the honest price he earns for exactly the same fruit. Tourism, too, has breathed surprising and welcome new life into sleepy villages that not long ago felt entirely forgotten by the wider world. (3)______ Travellers who are tired of crowded shopping malls increasingly long for clean mountain air, slow unhurried mornings, and the simple, almost forgotten pleasure of picking vegetables straight from the warm ground, and many are now quite willing to pay well for it. Just as importantly, a small but steadily growing number of well-educated young people are now actively choosing to return home for good. (4)______ Armed with valuable new skills in design, marketing, and modern farming science, these returning sons and daughters are bravely starting small businesses and reopening the local schools that had nearly closed their doors forever. None of this remarkable transformation, of course, ever happens entirely on its own. (5)______ Patient government support, fair new roads, honest local leaders, and the deep, stubborn determination of the villagers themselves must all somehow come together before a tired old village can truly begin to bloom again.
bank: Crumbling old farmhouses are now being lovingly repaired and reopened as warm, cosy guesthouses for curious and weary city visitors.
With one good signal, even the most ordinary villagers can suddenly sell their humble goods to the whole wide and hungry nation online.
Fields slowly lay untended, the village schools shut their heavy doors, and a quiet, settled sadness drifted over the empty lanes.
Abandoning the whole countryside completely and simply moving everyone into a few giant cities is clearly the wisest plan of all.
Real and lasting revival, the recent experience of these places clearly shows, always depends on many separate pieces fitting carefully together.
Every single successful village, the lazy story goes, simply waited around passively for good luck to arrive without anyone lifting a finger.
They bring back home with them not only fresh youthful energy but also valuable practical knowledge gained in faraway universities and firms.
sets.explain_zh: 空1填索引2（田地荒废学校关门，承接乡村衰落）；空2填索引1（网络让村民线上销往全国）；空3填索引0（老农舍改民宿，承接旅游）；空4填索引6（返乡青年带回知识与活力）；空5填索引4（振兴靠多方合力，收尾）。干扰句索引3（全搬进城）、索引5（被动等运气）放入任一空都不连贯。

sets.passage: When schools and universities first began moving many of their regular courses onto the open internet, plenty of confident people loudly predicted that the traditional brick-and-mortar classroom would soon vanish from the world entirely. (1)______ Many years later, the actual reality has turned out to be far more interesting, and far more carefully balanced, than either the loudest early fans or the harshest stubborn critics ever genuinely expected. The single greatest gift of online learning, almost everyone now readily agrees, is the remarkable way in which it tears down the old, stubborn walls of distance and cost. (2)______ A determined and curious student living in a small, remote town can now quietly sit in on detailed lectures given by famous professors thousands of miles away, paying very little, or sometimes nothing at all, for the rare privilege. Flexibility is another genuine and underrated advantage that suits a great many busy and complicated modern lives. (3)______ A tired working parent, a night-shift nurse, or a constantly travelling salesperson can finally study quietly at midnight or at dawn, fitting real education neatly around the stubborn and crowded edges of a demanding daily schedule. Yet online learning, for all of its obvious strengths, also clearly carries some serious and rather stubborn weaknesses of its own. (4)______ Sitting all alone in front of a glowing screen for many long hours, with no classmates sitting nearby and no teacher quietly watching, demands a high level of self-discipline that many honest learners simply struggle to maintain for long. The wisest path forward, most thoughtful educators now firmly believe, is not to choose rigidly and finally between the two separate worlds. (5)______ Blending the freedom and astonishing reach of online tools with the warmth and gentle accountability of real face-to-face teaching tends, in practice, to give most students the genuine best of both at once.
bank: Truly reliable internet access would, they argued, make every old form of classroom teaching completely and instantly useless overnight.
Almost anyone with even a cheap connection can suddenly reach valuable knowledge that was once locked away behind expensive closed gates.
Such bold and sweeping predictions, like so many confident forecasts about new technology, soon proved to be greatly and embarrassingly exaggerated.
The simple freedom to learn at any hour and from almost any place quietly removes the barriers that once stopped countless eager people.
All online study should really be banned entirely, some insist, because no real good can ever possibly come from learning through a screen.
The lonely, silent format makes it dangerously easy to slowly fall behind, lose focus, and then quietly give up on the whole thing altogether.
Thoughtfully combining the real strengths of both methods, rather than stubbornly forcing a single choice, usually serves ordinary learners far better.
sets.explain_zh: 空1选C（大胆预言被夸大，承接传统课堂将消失之说）；空2选B（廉价网络打破知识门槛）；空3选D（随时随地学习的灵活性）；空4选F（独自面屏易掉队失焦，承接弱点）；空5选G（融合两者优势更好）。干扰句A（网络让课堂彻底无用）、E（应全面禁止网课）放入任一空都不连贯。

sets.passage: Table manners may at first seem like a small and rather old-fashioned topic in our fast and busy modern world, and yet they quietly shape the way other people see us at almost every single shared meal. (1)______ Far from being merely a set of empty, fussy, and outdated rules, good manners at the table are really a simple and graceful language of respect that everyone seated around us can instantly understand. Different cultures, of course, faithfully follow strikingly different customs, and what counts as perfectly polite in one place may seem quite strange or even rude somewhere else entirely. (2)______ In some countries a loud and satisfied slurp cheerfully tells the cook that the hot soup is truly delicious, while in many others that very same sound would make every other guest at the table quietly wince. Despite all of these many colourful and confusing differences, a few gentle guiding principles seem to travel surprisingly well across most of the world's borders. (3)______ Patiently waiting until everyone at the table has been fully served before lifting your own fork, for example, is a small kindness that is understood and warmly appreciated almost anywhere on earth. The way we choose to behave toward those who quietly serve us matters just as deeply as which particular fork we happen to pick up. (4)______ A single warm and genuine word of thanks to a tired waiter, or to the kind host who has cooked patiently for hours, costs us absolutely nothing at all, yet it leaves behind a lasting impression of real human warmth. In the very end, the true heart of good table manners has remarkably little to do with rigid, anxious, or showy perfection. (5)______ It lies instead in a steady, thoughtful, and quiet awareness of the other people sharing your table, and in the simple, generous wish to make every single one of them feel comfortable and genuinely welcome.
bank: What seems perfectly normal and proper at one family's table can feel oddly rude at another's just a short distance across the sea.
How exactly we choose to eat quietly tells other people a great deal about how carefully we truly think of those beside us.
Eating as loudly and as quickly as you possibly can is somehow admired and richly rewarded in every single culture on earth.
Showing simple patience and honest gratitude toward others is warmly welcomed at tables in very nearly every corner of the whole globe.
Pointedly ignoring all the staff and loudly demanding much faster service is, some imagine, the clearest possible sign of true refinement.
Nobody seriously expects you to memorise every tiny rule, only to pay real attention to the comfort of all those quietly around you.
Treating both servers and generous hosts with plain everyday courtesy reveals far more about a person than any polished skill ever could.
sets.explain_zh: 空1填索引1（吃相体现对同桌的体贴，承接餐桌礼仪塑造印象）；空2填索引0（同一举止在不同文化差异巨大）；空3填索引3（耐心与感恩跨文化通行，承接共通原则）；空4填索引6（对服务者有礼更显修养）；空5填索引5（无需背熟规则，重在体贴他人，收尾）。干扰句索引2（越大声越快越受赞）、索引4（无视服务员才是高雅）放入任一空都不连贯。

sets.passage: There is a particular and quite unforgettable kind of joy in standing alone on a high mountain ridge at dawn, slowly watching the very first light spill across deep valleys that had seemed endless and impossible only a few short hours before. (1)______ Yet almost anyone who has ever truly climbed a serious peak will honestly tell you that this beautiful and hard-won reward is earned only through long hours of cold, aching, and sometimes genuinely frightening effort. Careful preparation, long before the very first step is ever actually taken, is what quietly separates the wise climber from the reckless and unlucky one. (2)______ Checking the day's weather forecast very carefully, packing more than enough warm layers and clean water, and clearly telling someone trustworthy exactly where you plan to go can quietly make all the difference between a fine day out and a terrible tragedy. The mountains, however stunningly beautiful they may appear, always demand a deep and completely honest respect from everyone who dares to enter them. (3)______ A sudden, violent storm can roll in over the ridge within just a few short minutes, swiftly turning a gentle, sunlit slope into a dangerous grey maze of howling wind, thick fog, and slippery, hidden stone. Truly wise climbers also slowly learn the surprisingly hard and humbling skill of turning back. (4)______ Pushing stubbornly and proudly toward the distant summit when your legs are already shaking and the daylight is quietly fading has ended far more lives high in the mountains than any single sudden, unlucky accident ever truly has. Above all, serious climbing patiently teaches a deep lesson that reaches far beyond the bare rock and ice themselves. (5)______ The hard-won patience, the quiet courage, and the steady inner calm that you slowly build on a difficult slope all quietly follow you home, helping you to face the steep and frightening challenges of ordinary daily life.
bank: Quiet, careful planning at the kitchen table the night before is honestly just as vital as having strong legs out on the steep trail.
Reaching that silent, windswept summit, you feel a clean and simple happiness that very few experiences down below can ever truly match.
Rushing carelessly up any high mountain with no real plan and no proper equipment at all is somehow the boldest and smartest way to climb.
Their weather can change with truly terrifying speed, harshly punishing anyone who foolishly treats these great heights too lightly.
Once you finally reach the very top of any hill, you can be sure that all real danger is completely and permanently behind you.
Knowing clearly when a stubborn goal has simply become far too dangerous to keep chasing is itself a real form of courage, not weakness.
Every hard and painful lesson learned high above the drifting clouds slowly becomes a quiet, lasting strength down in the valley far below.
sets.explain_zh: 空1填索引1（登顶的纯粹喜悦，承接黎明山脊之美）；空2填索引0（厨房里的周密计划与强壮双腿同样重要，承接准备）；空3填索引3（山中天气骤变需敬畏）；空4填索引5（懂得何时折返是勇气，承接学会回头）；空5填索引6（高山所学化为山下的力量，收尾）。干扰句索引2（无计划无装备最聪明）、索引4（登顶后危险全消）放入任一空都不连贯。

sets.passage: Most ordinary families quietly pay their monthly electricity bill each month with a small, tired sigh and remarkably little real thought about where all of that expensive energy actually goes inside their own busy homes. (1)______ Yet a genuinely surprising share of that hard-earned money simply leaks slowly away through careless habits and forgotten devices that quietly waste power both day and night, even while everyone in the house is fast asleep. Heating and cooling the house, in most ordinary households, swallow by far the single largest piece of the whole family energy budget. (2)______ Setting the thermostat just one or two degrees more sensibly, and carefully sealing the thin cold cracks around old doors and draughty windows, can trim a heavy bill noticeably without anyone in the family ever feeling cold or uncomfortable. Lighting, though a much smaller part of the bill, still offers some of the very easiest savings that any family can possibly find. (3)______ Quietly switching over to modern energy-saving bulbs, and simply remembering to flick off the lights in clearly empty rooms, costs almost nothing at all, and yet it adds up steadily across a long, cold, and dark winter. Many people are also genuinely startled to learn just how much power their idle, forgotten electronics quietly drink each day. (4)______ A television, a phone charger, or a game console left thoughtlessly plugged into the wall keeps sipping a thin, steady trickle of electricity even when it clearly appears to be switched fully off. Best of all, saving energy at home almost always means quietly saving real money too, with no genuine loss of comfort at all. (5)______ When a whole family slowly begins to notice all of these small hidden leaks and then gently closes them one by one, the lighter, friendlier bill at the very end of the month becomes a shared and deeply satisfying little victory.
bank: Simply leaving every single appliance in the house running constantly is, by far, the cleverest way for a family to keep its costs down.
Much of a typical home's wasted energy is lost not through dramatic obvious waste but through small, silent, unnoticed daily habits.
Keeping the heating turned on full blast all winter long, with the windows thrown wide open, is plainly the most efficient choice possible.
A poorly insulated old house quietly forces the tired heater or the air conditioner to work far harder and longer than it ever really should.
These quietly humming devices, often called energy vampires by experts, can secretly add a real and surprising sum to the whole yearly total.
Replacing tired old bulbs and faithfully turning off all unused lights are tiny daily actions that quietly pay off month after month.
These thoughtful saving habits also gently teach growing children, early on, that small careful choices genuinely do matter in the wider world.
sets.explain_zh: 空1选B（家中能源多因小习惯流失，承接很少思考能源去向）；空2选D（隔热差使冷暖设备空耗，承接供暖制冷占大头）；空3选F（换灯泡关灯月月省，承接照明易省）；空4选E（待机电器是吃电的能源吸血鬼）；空5选G（省能也教孩子小选择有意义）。干扰句A（让电器常开最省）、C（开窗全力供暖最高效）放入任一空都不连贯。

sets.passage: When we settle into our seats in a darkened cinema and lose ourselves completely in an unfolding story, it is remarkably easy to forget just how very much careful, patient craft lies hidden behind every single fleeting moment up on the bright screen. (1)______ A truly great film is never really the lonely work of one single isolated genius, but rather the remarkable result of hundreds of skilled and dedicated people all quietly pulling together in the very same chosen direction. The director, of course, sits firmly at the very centre of this enormous and complicated creative effort. (2)______ He or she must somehow hold the entire sweeping vision of the whole film firmly in mind at once, carefully guiding the actors, the designers, and dozens of others toward a single shared dream that exists, at the very beginning, only in the imagination. Yet the patient work of the camera itself is honestly just as crucial as the famous, glowing faces in front of it. (3)______ Where the lens quietly chooses to rest, exactly how the soft light falls across a single face, and how slowly or quickly a tense scene is cut together can shape our very deepest feelings without our ever once noticing precisely why. Sound, too, does far more quiet and invisible work than most ordinary audiences ever consciously realise during a film. (4)______ A slowly swelling piece of orchestral music, a sudden total silence, or the soft, distant patter of falling rain can quietly turn an otherwise ordinary scene into something that lingers vividly in the memory for many years. In the very end, perhaps this is the true and lasting magic of cinema as a serious art form. (5)______ Out of the long, patient labour of so many separate and skilful hands, one single living dream is carefully built, one that thousands of complete strangers can later quietly share together in the warm dark.
bank: A whole film succeeds entirely and completely because of its single most famous and most highly paid leading star.
What so often looks completely effortless up on the screen is, in honest truth, the careful product of countless small and deliberate decisions.
Every single camera angle is really a silent, deliberate choice, gently steering exactly what the watching audience notices and quietly feels.
Cutting the film's budget as brutally low as possible is somehow the surest known way to create a truly unforgettable lasting masterpiece.
It is always the patient director who slowly turns scattered ideas and many separate talents into one coherent and deeply moving whole.
The right swelling music, or one carefully placed sound effect, can quietly move a whole audience long before a single word is even spoken.
It gently gathers together the many gifts of so many different people and fuses them into one shared experience far larger than any of its parts.
sets.explain_zh: 空1选B（银幕上的轻松实为无数刻意决定，承接幕后匠心）；空2选E（导演把零散才能整合为一体）；空3选C（镜头角度是无声选择，引导观众感受，承接摄影）；空4选F（音乐音效先于台词打动观众，承接声音）；空5选G（电影汇聚众人之才成就更大体验）。干扰句A（全靠头牌明星）、D（压低预算造杰作）放入任一空都不连贯。
